tagline: Professional READMEs in seconds. No API costs. No setup.
value_proposition: ReadMeForge analyzes any public GitHub repository — detecting frameworks, package managers, CI configuration, Docker setup, and tooling — and generates a professional, structured README complete with relevant badges (license, framework, tests, Docker) in seconds. Template-driven engine means zero API costs and instant results. Supabase authentication and README history dashboard included.

primary.description: Developers actively maintaining OSS projects who lack bandwidth for documentation but know a poor README hurts adoption and contribution rates

secondary.segment: Indie hackers and side project developers
secondary.description: Builders shipping fast who treat documentation as a last step; want professional presentation without documentation effort

tertiary.description: New developers with 10–20 GitHub repos from coursework or projects; each repo needs a README; writing them manually is tedious and time-consuming

pain_points: Writing a README from scratch for every project is repetitive and time-consuming
Most developers don't know what a 'good' README should contain or how to structure it
Badges require manual configuration of shield.io URLs and are easy to get wrong
AI chat-based README generators require you to describe your project — ReadMeForge reads it
Existing tools produce generic output that doesn't reflect the actual tech stack
Portfolio repos with missing or minimal READMEs damage impression during hiring
OSS projects without clear setup instructions lose potential contributors at the first step

key_features.name: GitHub URL analysis
key_features.description: Paste any public GitHub repository URL — ReadMeForge fetches and analyzes the codebase structure without requiring authentication or cloning

key_features.name: Framework and stack detection
key_features.description: Automatically identifies frameworks (Next.js, React, Express, FastAPI, Django, etc.), package managers (npm, yarn, pnpm, pip, poetry), and runtime versions

key_features.name: Auto-generated badge suite
key_features.description: Produces correct shield.io badges for license, primary framework, Docker status, test runner, and deployment platform based on detected stack

key_features.name: Structured README sections
key_features.description: Generates Overview, Features, Tech Stack, Getting Started, Prerequisites, Installation, Usage, Contributing, and License sections based on what is actually present in the repo

key_features.name: Zero API costs
key_features.description: Template-driven generation engine — no LLM API call per README means instant results with no rate limits or cost-per-generation

key_features.name: README history dashboard
key_features.description: Supabase-backed authentication (magic link) with a personal dashboard showing all previously generated READMEs, filterable by repo

key_features.name: Viral attribution badge
key_features.description: 'Generated with ReadMeForge' badge embedded in output — links back to product, creating organic distribution on GitHub

tiers.name: Free
tiers.limits: 5 README generations per month, full output, Supabase history (last 5 entries)
tiers.target: Casual users, bootcamp students, one-time explorers

tiers.name: Pro
tiers.limits: Unlimited generations, full history, PDF export, custom badge sets, priority queue
tiers.target: Active OSS maintainers and indie hackers with multiple projects

tiers.name: Team
tiers.limits: Team workspaces, bulk generation via API, org-level history, Slack notifications
tiers.target: Engineering teams standardizing documentation across multiple repos

pricing_model.model_notes: Low friction free tier designed for viral GitHub distribution. Pro price point ($6) is below psychological resistance for developer tools. Team tier targets documentation standardization use case.

competitive_advantages: Zero API costs per generation — competitors using GPT-4 or Claude have cost-per-call that forces free tier restrictions
Reads real codebase structure — output is specific to the actual repo, not generic boilerplate
Auto-generates correct badges without manual shield.io URL construction
Supabase auth with magic link — no password friction, README history dashboard included in free tier
Viral loop built in — 'Generated with ReadMeForge' badge drives organic GitHub discovery
Veridux integration: RepoLens identifies repos with poor READMEs and routes them directly to ReadMeForge

competitive_landscape.key_differentiator_vs_competitors: Competitors either require manual template filling (readme.so) or free-form AI prompting (Copilot). ReadMeForge reads the repo and writes the README — no input required beyond the URL.

cross_promotion_targets.synergy: RepoLens health score flags repos with missing or low-quality READMEs and surfaces a direct CTA to ReadMeForge — closes the loop between 'diagnosis' and 'fix'
ReadMeForge as an AgenticNode workflow node: trigger README generation on every new repository creation or on a weekly schedule for active repos
Natural upsell: ReadMeForge generates the project README → prompt users to 'also generate your API reference docs with APIGhost'
Kynvo runs ReadMeForge's lifecycle email campaigns — onboarding series, free-to-pro upgrade sequences, re-engagement for inactive users
Portfolio homepage cross-link; Veridux newsletter covers ReadMeForge launch and feature updates; joint 'Veridux developer stack' positioning

estimated_tam: 100M+ GitHub repositories; 25M+ active GitHub developers; 4M+ OSS projects created annually
